title: Query Understanding, Filters, Rewriting, Expansion, and Decomposition · Behavior Case: Rag Hyde Hypothetical Document
summary: A model rewrites every query, drops quoted identifiers and dates, invents metadata filters, and issues six hidden searches when the user needed one clarification. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the hyde hypothetical document increment in bounded query-plan compiler. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Use hypothetical-document embedding only as an evaluated retrieval transform and never as evidence or a factual source.
Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-hyde-hypothetical-document — Use hypothetical-document embedding only as an evaluated retrieval transform and never as evidence or a factual source.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-query-understanding-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-query-understanding-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_query_understanding_5(query: str = "reset MFA for contractor", intent: str = "procedure", filters_valid: bool = True, ambiguity: int = 1, rewrite_count: int = 2, original_preserved: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if not query.strip() or not intent:
        return False, 0, "query-intent"
    if not filters_valid:
        return False, rewrite_count, "filter-schema"
    if ambiguity < 0 or rewrite_count < 0 or rewrite_count > 5:
        return False, rewrite_count, "rewrite-budget"
    if not original_preserved:
        return False, rewrite_count, "query-lineage"
    if ambiguity > 2:
        return False, rewrite_count, "clarification-required"
    assert rewrite_count <= 5
    return True, rewrite_count, "bounded-query-plan"
